title: CSS-DOS cart manifest
description: Declarative spec for a CSS-DOS cart. The builder reads this file (plus the cart's other contents) and produces a cabinet (a .css file runnable in Chrome, calcite-cli, or the static player).

name.description: Human-readable cart title. Defaults to the cart folder/zip basename.

author.description: Cart author. Optional, unused by the builder, preserved in the cabinet header.

description.description: Short cart description. Optional, unused by the builder, preserved in the cabinet header.

preset.description: Named preset. Supplies defaults for every other field; the cart's own values override the preset on conflict.

bios.description: Which BIOS to boot. Gossamer is the hack-path shim; Muslin is the assembly DOS BIOS (fallback); Corduroy is the structured C DOS BIOS (default).

conventional.description: Size of conventional RAM in bytes. String = preset; number = exact bytes; 'autofit' sizes to the program. NOTE: on DOS carts, values below 640K are accepted by the schema but are not currently known to boot — the builder warns. Tracked as a follow-up.

gfx.description: Include the VGA Mode 13h framebuffer at 0xA0000–0xAFA00 (64000 bytes). Required for Mode 13h programs and the Corduroy boot splash.

textVga.description: Include the VGA text buffer at 0xB8000–0xB8FA0 (4000 bytes). Required for any program that writes text via direct video memory (i.e. almost every DOS program).

cgaGfx.description: Include CGA graphics memory at 0xB8000–0xBC000 (16384 bytes). Covers modes 0x04 (320×200×4) and 0x06 (640×200×2). Overlaps the text buffer's address range — enabling both is fine, the bytes share storage the same way real CGA hardware shares the 16 KB aperture between text and graphics.

mode.description: 'rom' exposes the disk outside 8086 memory via a 512-byte window (the current default). 'embedded' bakes disk bytes into 8086 memory (hacky, documented footnote, only works for tiny disks).

size.description: Floppy size. String = standard floppy preset; number = exact bytes; 'autofit' = round up to the smallest preset that fits. NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — today the floppy size is hard-coded in mkfat12.mjs.

writable.description: Whether INT 13h can write to the disk (Corduroy AH=03h). Writes live in shadow cells for the lifetime of the tab; reloading resets to the factory floppy. Opt-in: the shadow adds one memory cell per disk byte, growing the cabinet by roughly the disk size in cells — game carts should leave it off.

files.description: Explicit disk contents. If omitted, the builder auto-discovers files from the cart folder (every file except program.json). KERNEL.SYS, COMMAND.COM, ANSI.SYS, and CONFIG.SYS are always added automatically.

name.description: 8.3 filename as it appears on the floppy (uppercased).

source.description: Path relative to the cart root. Must not escape via '..'.

cover.description: Website only. Boxart filename resolved against the site's boxart directory (web/site/public/assets/boxart/) — not a path inside the cart, never lands on the floppy. Presence opts the cart into the featured landing grid. Ignored by the builder.

bullets.description: Website only. Cover-less featured card: the site renders 'NAME with:' followed by these lines on the accent colour. Presence (like cover) opts the cart into the featured landing grid. Ignored by the builder.

accent.description: Website only. CSS background colour for the cover-less text card (default #0000AA). Ignored by the builder.

vsyncMode.description: Player paint cadence. 'sim' paints on the simulated 70 Hz vertical-retrace edge derived from the CPU cycle counter (matches what the guest program sees when it polls port 0x3DA). 'wall' paints on wall-clock 70 Hz regardless of CPU speed. 'turbo' paints every eval batch, no throttling. NOT YET PLUMBED — today the mode is picked by ?vsync=... on the player URL or the status-bar dropdown; this field records the cart's preferred default for future builder/player wiring.

os.description: DOS carts only. Which DOS boots. "edrdos" (default): the EDR-DOS kernel is preloaded at 0060:0000 and jumped to directly. "msdos4": real MS-DOS 4.00 (dos/msdos4/, MIT-licensed) boots from the floppy's own MSBOOT boot sector via the BIOS INT 19h bootstrap; IO.SYS/MSDOS.SYS/COMMAND.COM are placed on the disk and nothing is preloaded. Requires the corduroy BIOS. boot.runCommand becomes a line in a synthesized AUTOEXEC.BAT instead of a SHELL /K argument; boot.ems is not supported.

runCommand.description: DOS carts only. The exact command line passed to COMMAND.COM via /K, e.g. 'DOOM -noxms -nosound'. Empty string drops to the COMMAND.COM prompt with no command run. CONFIG.SYS always emits SHELL=\COMMAND.COM /P /K <runCommand>; the cart never runs as the shell directly. Default: if the cart contains exactly one .COM/.EXE, defaults to its name (no extension); otherwise empty.

raw.description: Hack carts only. Filename of the .COM to load raw at 0x100. Mutually exclusive with boot.runCommand.

ems.description: DOS carts only. When true, load a fake EMS device driver (EMSDRV.SYS) so programs that detect EMS via open("EMMXXXX0") see EMS as available. Doesn't provide real EMS pages — gates only. Needed for DOOM8088 and similar EMS-required programs.
